item.title: US Tells ASML It’s Concerned China May Have Top Chip Tool
item.description: Dutch chip-equipment giant ASML Holding NV is contending with its biggest challenge yet under the Trump administration: In a series of recent meetings, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick outlined concerns to ASML’s senior leaders that one of its top-of-the-line machines may have made its way into China, in violation of US-led export restrictions.

item.title: S. Korean Producer Prices Rise Most Since 2022 on Energy Costs
item.description: South Korea’s producer prices rose in May at the fastest pace in nearly four years, as higher energy costs and a broad-based increase in industrial prices continued to filter through the economy.

item.title: BOJ’s Himino Notes Risk of Price Trend Rising Above 2% Target
item.description: There’s a risk that Japan’s price trend could accelerate above the central bank’s 2% inflation target, Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino said as he explained the rationale for the interest rate hike earlier in the week.

item.title: US Nuclear Pilot Program Notches Second Reactor Breakthrough
item.description: Valar Atomics Inc., a Southern California-based startup, has reached a key milestone in its effort to develop small reactors under a US program aimed at accelerating the deployment of nuclear power.

item.title: BHP Shares Fall After $2.3 Billion Writedown on Potash Mine
item.description: BHP Group shares fell after the company flagged a $2.3 billion writedown on its giant potash mine in Canada due to cost and time overruns for an expansion of the project.

item.title: MSCI Flags South Korea Market Hurdles Ahead of Key Review
item.description: South Korea is yet to resolve several key obstacles facing foreign investors, MSCI Inc. said in its latest market accessibility review, a setback for global funds and the government hoping the country’s reform drive could pave the way toward a future upgrade to developed-market status.

item.title: Gold Set for Weekly Loss as Hawkish Fed Outweighs Peace Deal
item.description: Gold was on track for a third weekly loss, as a hawkish Federal Reserve and rate-hike bets outweighed the signing of an interim peace deal between the US and Iran.

item.title: Extenet Warns Cash to Run Out in Days Without ABS Holder Relief
item.description: Telecom-infrastructure firm Extenet is facing a cash crunch that could force it to cease operations before the end of the month, according to a letter shared with the company’s bond investors.

item.title: Japan Push for Corporate Growth Over Value Sets Off Skepticism
item.description: The Japanese government’s new push to encourage companies to use their cash for longer-term growth is raising concerns it will lead to unprofitable investments and erode corporate value.

item.title: Japan’s Inflation Holds Steady as Subsidies Restrain Energy
item.description: Japan’s key inflation gauge held steady in May thanks in part to government subsidies that kept energy costs down, keeping the Bank of Japan on track for another interest-rate hike later this year.

item.title: CFTC Not Ready for Prediction Market Social Cost, State AGs Say
item.description: Attorneys general for California and Minnesota said the Commodity Futures Trading Commission isn’t equipped to handle gambling-associated issues like addiction, highlighting challenges the federal regulator faces as it aims to assert authority over prediction markets.

item.title: Confidence Among Young Britons Slides to Lowest in Two Years
item.description: Confidence among Britons aged 16 to 29 sank to the lowest in two years, as the young bear the brunt of a weak UK labor market.

item.title: Philippine, Thai Earnings Southeast Asia’s Worst Hit by Iran War
item.description: Philippine and Thai companies are bearing the brunt of earnings downgrades across Southeast Asia, as their economies rely heavily oil and gas choked off by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Elliott, SVP Buy Braskem Debt Amid Restructuring Talks
item.description: Elliott Investment Management LP and SVP Global have recently bought debt of Braskem SA, the Brazilian petrochemical producer rushing to get creditors to agree to a restructuring plan, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Standard Nuclear Files for US IPO In Reactor Fuel Push
item.description: Standard Nuclear Inc., a maker of fuel for nuclear reactors, filed for an initial public offering as the power needs to fund the artificial intelligence boom continue to grow.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Rise as US-Iran Deal Lifts Mood: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were poised to rise Friday after a peace deal between the US and Iran boosted optimism that the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz will ease inflation pressures by restoring oil flows.

item.title: SpaceX Criticizes EU Proposal for Satellite Spectrum Tender
item.description: SpaceX criticized the European Union’s plans for the distribution of satellite spectrum, a key element in the growth plans for the Elon Musk-led company’s Starlink service following its record-setting initial public offering.

item.title: Oil Set for Deep Weekly Loss as Hormuz Traffic Starts to Pick Up
item.description: Oil headed for a substantial weekly drop as the US-Iran interim peace deal saw shipping through the Strait of Hormuz start to return to normal, easing the global crude market’s biggest ever supply shock.

item.title: MSCI Says Flow of Market Information Worsening in Indonesia
item.description: Information flow has deteriorated in Indonesian markets, MSCI Inc. said ahead of a potential downgrade that could further compound issues for the world’s worst-performing equities.

item.title: Candle’s Mayer Says Ad-Backed Streaming ‘Snuck Up’ on Industry
item.description: Fox Corp.’s $22 billion acquisition of Roku Inc. illustrates how free-to-watch, ad-supported streaming services are becoming a major force in the entertainment industry, according to Candle Media Inc. co-Chief Executive Officer Kevin Mayer.

item.title: Warsh Rattles Markets With Inflation Vow They Expect Him to Keep
item.description: Everyone knew Kevin Warsh planned to stop holding the market’s hand when he took over as Federal Reserve chairman. They were less prepared to be jolted awake at his very first press conference by the sound of a committed hawk who views taming inflation as his biggest job.

item.title: Schwab Warns of Margin Calls as Concerns Over Tax Bets Mount
item.description: Charles Schwab Corp. is imposing new margin requirements for clients using long-short investment strategies, adding more guardrails around a fast-growing tax-management trade.

item.title: Montreal Firms Pitch $177 Million Tower to Lure NATO-Linked Bank
item.description: Canada’s largest cities are competing to host the future headquarters of a specialized bank for defense lending, and developers in Montreal are trying to boost that region’s bid with a proposed C$250 million ($177 million) building to house it.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: ホルムズ封鎖解除、インテルとアップル、スペースX社債発行検討、アマゾン半導体、JERA

item.title: SpaceX’s Epic Fundraising Campaign for AI Has Only Just Begun
item.description: When Elon Musk went into sales pitch mode ahead of the SpaceX IPO, the comparison he marshaled to justify valuing the company in the trillions of dollars, and tapping investors for tens of billions, raised a few eyebrows.

item.title: Google Says Canada’s Data Law Changes Fail to Ease Concerns
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google said Canada’s changes to a proposed law that would help police obtain citizen data from private companies don’t resolve many of its concerns.

item.title: Meta Strikes New AI Computing Deals With Data Center Firm Crusoe
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has secured new agreements to get AI computing power from data center developer Crusoe, bolstering the infrastructure it needs to support an ambitious artificial intelligence expansion.

item.title: KPMG Australia Scandal Heats Up With Reckoning in Parliament
item.description: The turmoil engulfing KPMG Australia is set for a public reckoning as lawmakers demand information and changes in an industry beset by a series of scandals around the misuse of confidential client information.

item.title: ICE Reverses Plan to Use Mega-Warehouses for Migrant Detention
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is backing away from a controversial plan to turn empty warehouses into immigration detention centers and will keep relying largely on existing jails run by private contractors and state and local partners.

item.title: Dog Recovers From Screwworm But Origin Puzzles Officials
item.description: A New Mexico dog infected with the New World screwworm has recovered, but questions remain over how the animal contracted the parasite.
